action.hypothesis: With `routed_lowrank_two_stage_residual` clearly weaker than the current `lowrank_two_stage_residual` K128 anchor, the next and only justified matched architecture probe is `lowrank_residual_vq`. It preserves the same low-rank coarse-trunk intuition and static-library export contract while swapping staged residual selection for a harder discrete coarse assignment. If that discrete variant cannot materially challenge the `lowrank_two_stage_residual` anchor under the exact same `K=128, EF=12, signum, LR=2.0e-3` recipe, then hard residual-VQ is not worth continuing in the current LUTurbo-aligned regime.
action.summary: Run one matched architecture probe by switching only `ARCHITECTURE` to `lowrank_residual_vq` while keeping the mainline recipe fixed at `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, and the baseline loss/runtime settings unchanged. Judge the probe by the full curve, not just endpoint FVU: if `lowrank_residual_vq` stays smooth and materially pressures the `lowrank_two_stage_residual` anchor, it remains worth keeping in reserve; if it is clearly weaker or less stable, close the family-worthiness question and return to the `lowrank_two_stage_residual` mainline next round.
action.self_review: This obeys the Round 85 matched-probe rule: one probe only, one main variable only, and `K`, `OPTIMIZER`, `LR`, and `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` remain fixed. I chose `lowrank_residual_vq` because it is a genuinely different but still LUTurbo-compatible decomposition pattern with stronger prior signal than simpler selector-only families. The main risk is that hard assignment raises brittleness and may underperform on curve smoothness even if early descent looks acceptable, which is why the full trajectory matters more than the final scalar alone.
notes_to_memory: Round 85 uses the single allowed matched architecture probe on `lowrank_residual_vq` against the exact `lowrank_two_stage_residual` K128/EF12/signum/LR=2.0e-3 anchor.
Interpret this as a family-worthiness screen only: because hard residual-VQ can add discrete-selection brittleness, only a clear and stable quality advantage justifies future follow-up.
next_hypotheses: Return to the `lowrank_two_stage_residual` mainline next round regardless, per the operator instruction.
If `lowrank_residual_vq` is clearly worse or less stable, keep focus on mainline loss/preprocess clarification rather than reopening more architecture variants immediately.
If it wins clearly, note it as a reserve candidate but still resume mainline recipe clarification next round before any further architecture churn.
